data.title: Tencent Stock (TCEHY) Slips on Reports It Could Get the TikTok Treatment
data.description:
Tencent (TCEHY) stock fell on Wednesday alongside reports that the U.S. might force it to give up on its video game investments in the country. According to a report from the Financial Times, the Trump administration could give Tencent the same treatment it has given ByteDance, which has been forced to divest TikTok due to alleged security threats.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

If the Trump administration moves forward with plans to make Tencent divest its U.S. video game investments, it could mark a major change for the entertainment company. Tencent has heavily invested in video game companies over the past few years, including its 28% stake in Fortnite developer Epic Games. The company also outright owns League of Legends developer Riot Games, and Back 4 Blood and Left 4 Dead developer Turtle Rock Studios. Tencent has investments in several other game studios as well, including some in Europe and Japan.

Several of the Trump administration's top Cabinet members have reportedly met to discuss the potential security risk that Tencent's gaming investments present to the U.S. Another internal meeting was set for Tuesday, but was reportedly delayed. The results of this meeting could drastically affect Tencent and the video game sector.

Tencent Stock Movement Today

Tencent stock was down 1.72% on Wednesday, extending a 16.29% drop year-to-date. The shares have also fallen 0.96% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Dow Jones Climbs on Unexpected U.S.-Iran War Update
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Wednesday on higher ground following a report that Iran reached out to the U.S. about peace talks.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Officials from Iran's Ministry of Intelligence indirectly reached out to the CIA one day after the U.S. and Israel launched an attack on the country over the weekend, according to the New York Times. However, U.S. officials remain doubtful that either the Trump administration or Iran is prepared to de-escalate, with strikes continuing from both sides.

Private Employment Growth Tops Estimates

Elsewhere, the U.S. labor market received a positive signal after ADP reported that private employers added 63,000 jobs in February, the most since July 2025 and above the estimate of 50,000. At the same time, hiring remains concentrated in certain sectors, with education and health services contributing 58,000 job additions, while professional and business services lost 30,000 jobs.

"But with hiring concentrated in only a few sectors, our data shows no widespread pay benefit from changing jobs," said ADP Chief Economist Dr. Nela Richardson. "In fact, the pay premium for switching employers hit a record low in February."

Investors will get another snapshot of the labor market when the Department of Labor publishes jobless claims data on Thursday morning.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Buy Nvidia Shares Ahead of GTC, Says Wells Fargo
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) stock might be showing little momentum right now but if history is anything to go by, that might all change soon.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The chip giant's upcoming GTC 2026 event is scheduled for March 16‑19. Historically, the stock has tended to perform strongly after GTC, with Nvidia typically outperforming the SOX index by around 30% over the three months following the event, ranging from roughly 12% to 45% over the past few years.

Hence, says Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers: "We're NVDA buyers ahead of the event."

Among the items Rakers will be looking out for are updates regarding the pipeline. With commitments already exceeding $500 billion and potentially stretching into calendar 2027, Rakers estimates Nvidia has recognized roughly $240 billion to $250 billion of that opportunity since Blackwell began shipping in late 2024. An update to $600 billion+ would be a positive.

Additionally, Rakers, who ranks among the top 1% of analysts on Wall Street, will be watching for updates on Nvidia's move to the Kyber architecture – its next-generation, high-density rack design – and how lessons from the early GB200 NVL72 rollout might shape that transition. He'll also be looking to see whether Feynman, Nvidia's planned post-Rubin GPU architecture, represents a completely new approach or just an evolution of existing designs.

Rakers will also be keeping an eye on Rubin CPX, Nvidia's GPU built for long‑context AI inference and expected to launch in late 2026, as well as how the company positions its Groq LPU for AI workloads. He's looking for more detail on Rubin CPX's architecture and how Nvidia plans to integrate Groq's technology, particularly its large on-chip SRAM and streaming design that helps keep inference fast and low-latency.

There could be some "wildcards" thrown in the mix too. The 5-star analyst points out that AI infrastructure growth reflects Jevons Paradox – as compute gets more powerful, usage expands, driving more token generation. Within this context, Groq integration vs. new inference-focused silicon could be in focus. There has been speculation about whether Nvidia's Groq IP agreement will lead to standalone chips or if the technology will be incorporated into the company's future GPU roadmap.

Another wildcard focuses on the prospect of customization. AMD + Meta's recent chiplet-based partnership highlights the potential for custom AI hardware for hyperscalers. Nvidia's Blackwell and Rubin GPUs already use a chiplet/dielet architecture, with Vera-Rubin Ultra expanding this further. The Wall Street Journal has reported that Nvidia plans a dedicated inference chip for OpenAI, leveraging its Groq IP license to optimize AI inference. According to the report, OpenAI has committed to significant purchases, making customized AI hardware a potential wildcard for Nvidia's competitive positioning.

All told, Rakers maintained an Overweight (i.e., Buy) rating on the shares, backed by a $265 price target. The implication for investors? Upside of 47% from current levels. (To watch Rakers' track record, click here)

38 other analysts join Rakers in the bull camp, overpowering 1 Hold and Sell, each, and all adding up to a Strong Buy consensus rating. The forecast calls for one-year returns of 51%, considering the average target clocks in at $271.89. (See Nvidia stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Alphabet (GOOGL) Faces Lawsuit Over Gemini Chatbot in First AI Wrongful Death Case
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL) faces a new lawsuit that could pose fresh risks to the fast-growing AI field. The lawsuit claims the firm's Gemini chatbot led a Florida man into a deep bond with the system and then pushed him toward self-harm. The case was filed in a U.S. court in Northern California and may be the first wrongful death claim tied to Google's AI tools.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For tech firms now racing to launch new AI apps, the case may also draw more attention to safety rules and the legal risks tied to AI chat systems.

Meanwhile, GOOGL shares dropped nearly 1% on Tuesday, closing at $303.58.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Stock Futures Recover as Crude Oil Prices Fall for the First Time Since U.S.-Iran conflict
data.description:
Stock futures trended higher on Wednesday morning despite the escalating U.S.-Iran conflict. Oil prices fell after Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent assured that the U.S. would make several announcements to ensure the flow of oil through the Persian Gulf. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.51%, 0.30%, and 0.23%, respectively, at 8:30 a.m. EST on March 4.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower after early-session plunges marked some of their worst declines in years. The S&P 500 closed down 0.94%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite and the Dow Jones fell 1.02% and 0.83%, respectively.

President Trump stated last night that the U.S. Navy stands ready to escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz despite Iran's actions. Nonetheless, oil prices continued rising on supply fears, with Brent crude up 2.55% at $83.95 per barrel, near its highest since January 2025.

Turning to trending stocks, Moderna (MRNA) shares rose more than 9% in Wednesday's pre-market trading as the biotechnology company agreed to pay up to $2.25 billion to settle a Covid vaccine patent lawsuit with Biopharma Corporation and Genevant Sciences GmbH.

Meanwhile, GitLab stock fell more than 11% after the software company issued a disappointing Fiscal 2027 outlook. In contrast, Ross Stores (ROST) stock jumped 7% after the off-price retailer reported upbeat fiscal first-quarter results.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Oil Prices Jump after Iran Claims Control of Strait of Hormuz — 3 Top Energy Stocks to Watch Now
data.description:
Oil prices moved higher after Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it has taken control of the Strait of Hormuz and warned ships not to pass through the route. The development raised new concerns about global oil supply and increased fears of a wider conflict in the Middle East.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Following the development, Brent crude prices extended a four-day rally, rising 2.65% to $83.60 per barrel on Wednesday, hovering near the highest level since January 2025. U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude also climbed for a third straight session to $76.45. The rise in prices is putting major oil producers such as Devon Energy (DVN), SLB (SLB) and Diamondback Energy (FANG) in focus.

Why the Strait of Hormuz Matters

Iran lies next to the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway that carries a large share of the world's oil shipments. The route handles a significant portion of global crude exports, making it one of the most important chokepoints in the energy market.

Because so much oil passes through this corridor, any threat to tanker traffic can quickly push prices higher. Analysts said that if tanker traffic remains limited, oil prices could stay elevated in the near term. As a result, we identified two "Strong Buy" energy companies using the TipRanks database that could benefit from the rise in oil prices.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
